steps.title: Identify the logical relationship
steps.content: The logical relationship is cause-effect. The blank needs a transition signaling that John Francis earned his historical place by means of his military service — his service was the cause, and the historical recognition is the effect.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — "in doing so": signals that the result (earning a place in history) came about through the action just described (serving in the Civil War), so fits. ✓
  • Choice B — "for instance": signals exemplification — it would mean his service is a specific example of a general claim made earlier, but no such general claim appears. ✗
  • Choice C — "in any case": signals concession or dismissal of prior points, implying his historical significance is true regardless of some complication. No complication exists here. ✗
  • Choice D — "usually": signals habitual action, not a result. It would imply he typically earned historical recognition in such situations, which is nonsensical. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
